summary: Post‑Pig Pen Lovelight jam and lively Touch of Grey highlight the show
blurb: Turn On Your Lovelight shines with a soulful post‑Pig Pen jam, followed by an upbeat big‑band rendition of Touch of Grey after Jerry’s award speeches. The band maintains solid energy throughout the brief set, and recording quality is generally good.
review:
The March 12, 1988 concert at San Francisco’s Civic Auditorium captured a memorable moment in Dead history. After two heartfelt acceptance speeches by Jerry Garcia, the band launched into a stirring post‑Pig Pen “Turn On Your Lovelight,” showcasing Brent’s tasteful organ textures and tight rhythm from Mickey and Billy. The set closed with a fun, big‑band take on “Touch of Grey,” keeping the crowd upbeat and adding an extra layer of celebration to the night. While the recording is listed as unknown source type, reviewers noted no major sound issues, delivering a clean listening experience that lets the band’s interplay shine.

Overall this show offers a concise but special snapshot of the late‑80s Dead, highlighted by Jerry’s eloquent speeches and the band’s ability to turn a short set into a joyous celebration.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All available analysis expresses positive sentiment, praising the post‑Pig Pen Lovelight jam and the big‑band Touch of Grey, with no criticism of performance quality.

best_recording.reason: Good overall sound quality with no noted issues and captures the highlighted Lovelight and Touch of Grey moments clearly.

key_highlights: Post‑Pig Pen “Turn On Your Lovelight” delivers a soulful jam.
Big‑band “Touch of Grey” adds fun energy after Jerry’s speeches.
Jerry’s humble acceptance speeches give the concert an emotional touch.
Recording is generally good with no major sound problems reported.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers humble yet eloquent acceptance speeches that add poignancy; his playing supports the special moments.
